headline: Survey at 15% completion with strong regional concentration; urgent scaling needed.
collection_status: 199 of 1,350 responses collected (14.7% of target). Three regions (Lindi, Tanga, Mara) account for 78% of responses, leaving 7 regions severely underrepresented. Current pace suggests 6+ months to target without intervention.
key_findings: Teachers dominate respondent pool (101/199, 51%), but critical district/regional education officials remain sparse (6 DEOs, 2 REOs, 1 other region-level). Leadership buy-in signals weak.
Expansion appetite is clear: 71 respondents prioritise expanding KiuFunza, with 40 flagging policy integration as essential—aligns with sustainability messaging but execution gap evident.
Scale readiness score of 2.7/5 contradicts expansion demand; suggests respondents want growth but lack confidence in systems/capacity to deliver it.
Feedback reveals three recurring themes: teacher motivation/incentives, digital monitoring systems, and government policy alignment—operationally specific, not aspirational.
flags: Geographic collapse: Songwe (8), Singida (1), and Mtwara (12) combined = 21 responses. Risk of regional bias in findings and weak stakeholder engagement outside core zones.
Senior official under-representation: Only 6 DEOs and 2 REOs responding. Without district/regional leadership buy-in, policy and scale recommendations will lack credibility.
Mismatch between aspiration (expand KiuFunza) and confidence (2.7 readiness score): Respondents want growth but doubt organisational capacity—suggests training, resource, or trust deficits.
recommendation: Immediately redirect collection effort to underrepresented regions (Songwe, Singida, Mtwara) and prioritise DEO/REO outreach through regional directors—these voices are essential for credible scaling recommendations. Simultaneously, conduct rapid pulse interviews on the readiness gap to understand barriers behind the 2.7 score before final analysis.
